answer:
Karma is framed differently in each smṛti, depending on whether the text emphasises mechanism, embodiment, or accounting.

1. Manu (Manusmṛti, chapter 12) gives the doctrine in its most schematic form. Mn_12.51: 'a movement of beings, of three kinds, threefold and threefold again, has been declared for the three kinds of action' (p-074f6e). Karma here is a typology — actions are classified by mind, speech, body and by sattva/rajas/tamas, and each type drives a corresponding rebirth-trajectory. Action becomes consequence through a quasi-physical mechanism of guṇa-coloured deed → guṇa-coloured outcome.

2. Yājñavalkya (Yj. ch.3) makes karma about embodiment. Yj_3.155 (p-568993): 'Of such conduct, with an undisciplined self and adhering to falsehood, he is bound by karma, by aversion and delusion, and also by desire.' And the metaphor at Yj_3.162 (p-b66c2a): 'Just as an actor depicts his own person with various colors, creating many forms, so the Self takes on bodies born of karma.' Karma here is the principle that drives the Self into successive bodies. The accent is on bondage and the path out of it — Yj_3.160 (p-f20b7d) describes the wearing-away (kṣaya) of karma as a precondition for yoga.

3. Viṣṇu Smṛti (chs 43–44) treats karma operationally as a balance sheet. Ch.43 enumerates the twenty-eight narakas (Tāmisra, Andhatāmisra, Raurava…, Vi_43.1–43.28, opening at p-6ae760). Ch.44 then maps post-hell rebirth: 'for sinful souls who have experienced suffering in the hells, there are births as animals' (Vi_44.1, p-0990c4). The fit between sin and womb is mechanical and tabulated — heinous sinners → immovable beings, great sinners → worms, minor sinners → aquatic animals, etc. Karma here is administrative: every sin is paired with a hell and a subsequent re-embodiment.

Where does action become consequence? In Manu, in the gradient from saatvika to tāmasic action. In Yājñavalkya, at the moment the Self assumes a new body. In Viṣṇu, on a precise schedule administered by Yama. The same word names a metaphysical principle (Yj), a typological mechanism (Mn), and a penal calendar (Vi).

tool_calls.name: get_passage
tool_calls.result_summary: Yj_3.155 (bondage by karma) and Yj_3.162 (Self takes on bodies born of karma)
